dialogos.texto_en: Oh?\nWhat are you doing over here?
Ah, it's Uesugi-kun.\nAre you here to see the flowers too?
No, I just happened to come this way.\nBut, flowers...
This is the Tropical Dream Center?\nSo, it's a botanical garden?
Yes, there's a large greenhouse here.\nYou can see not only rare tropical plants\nbut also flowers all year round.
There are orchids and hibiscus\nin full bloom, and it smells so good.
Oh, I see. So you've already seen it all.
Heheh, seems like bad timing.\nI just finished seeing it.
It was perfect for taking a leisurely walk,\nsurrounded by all the flowers and greenery.
That's right, Uesugi-kun. When you think of Okinawa,\ndon't you imagine a hibiscus to represent it?
Oh, yeah.\nIt's often used in designs too, isn't it?
Well then, here's a question.\nWhat is the 'Prefectural flower' of Okinawa?
No idea.
Aren't you giving up too soon?\nOr rather, you haven't even thought about it,\nhave you?
So, what's the answer?
Come on...\nCouldn't you at least stick it out a little longer?
The correct answer is the deigo flower.
Deigo...\nI feel like I've heard of it before, but I'm not sure.
Wild guess, hibiscus?
That's what you would normally think, right?
...The way you're saying it\nmakes me think it's not, though.
Yes, that's incorrect.
The correct answer is the deigo flower.
Deigo...\nI feel like I've heard of it before, but I'm not sure.
Is this a trick question?\nIt can't be hibiscus, then.
Of course you wouldn't fall for it.\nThen what flower is the correct answer?
I...don't know.
The correct answer is the deigo flower.
Deigo...\nI feel like I've heard of it before, but I'm not sure.
I also learned about it for the first time\nwhile reading the descriptions during the tour.
In Okinawa, it is said that in years\nwith particularly strong typhoons,\ndeigo flowers bloom in droves.
Oh, I see.\nHearing something like that makes me feel \nlike those flowers are deeply rooted in this land.
I wonder how it will be this year.
Typhoons, eh...\nI hope they won't be too rough.
Our storm is about to come, though.
But once it passes, sunny days will return.
Ah, I see, it's like the calm after the storm.
Yes...\nI'm sure a refreshing blue sky\nis waiting for us.
